planPrerequisites.description: pact-write must consume the persisted plan snapshot from disk before deciding what provider-verification coverage slice is safe to implement.

scanSupportInputs.description: pact-write must read the persisted scan snapshot from disk as supporting evidence for target files, coverage gaps, interaction grounding, provider-state hints, and artifact-source honesty.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical persisted write result for coverage-aware Pact provider verification. It must capture the selected provider, the input plan verdict, the targeted coverage slice, actual writes performed, intentional skips, remaining uncovered areas, validation focus, manual follow-ups, and the next verification step without pretending readiness unsupported by persisted state.

planVerdictTaxonomy.description: pact-write must obey this persisted plan verdict taxonomy instead of inventing a new decision surface. The verdict defines write budget, but the concrete coverage slice must still come from persisted coverage evidence.

writeOutcomeTaxonomy.description: pact-write must distinguish real coverage extensions (`written`), preparatory or blocked partial work (`partial`), blocked writes, and honest no-op outcomes.

coverageWriteRules: prefer extension over recreation when an existing provider verification target already exists
record the targeted coverage slice explicitly: category, endpoints, interactions when grounded, and provider-state work
do not mark an endpoint or interaction as implemented merely because a generic harness exists
do not guess provider states that are not grounded in persisted interaction evidence
keep writes narrow and local to the next attributable coverage increment
preserve remaining uncovered endpoints, interaction gaps, and blockers explicitly in write-state

repoWritePolicy: extend existing provider verification files when safe
create new provider-verification files only when the persisted plan and repo evidence do not expose a safe existing extension point
add missing provider-state support only when grounded by persisted interaction evidence
allow minimal dependency/config patches only when clearly needed by the chosen coverage slice
forbid broad refactors or unrelated file rewrites
ownership.description: pact-write may always update the canonical write state files above and may touch repo files only when the persisted plan and repo evidence make the target narrow, attributable, and safe.

stopConditions.description: Required persisted plan state is missing, so write cannot proceed honestly.
Required persisted scan state is missing, so write cannot reconcile target files and coverage evidence honestly.
The persisted plan verdict is blocked, so write must not imply progress by generating scaffolding.
The persisted plan verdict is irrelevant, so Pact provider verification artifacts must not be written.
The safe provider verification target file or extension point cannot be identified without inventing a new architecture.

summaryState.description: Human-readable Pact write summary only. Canonical machine state stays in write-state.json.
